name: Garden Vegetable Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 3 cups coarsely chopped zucchini and/or yellow crookneck squash
1 1/2 cups fresh corn kernels or frozen, thawed
2 cups grated Monterey Jack and/or sharp cheddar (about 8 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add zucchini and onion, and sauté until vegetables are just tender, about 4 minutes. Mix in 1 cup corn, canned chilies, and 1/4 cup cilantro; season filling with salt and pepper.
3. Melt 3 tablespoons butter in a heavy medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Whisk in chili powder, flour, and cumin; stir for 30 seconds. Gradually whisk in milk. Cook until the sauce is thick and bubbling, whisking occasionally, about 5 minutes. Add 1 1/2 cups cheese; whisk until smooth. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Spread 1/4 cup sauce in the bottom of a 13 x 9 x 2-inch glass baking dish. Mix 3/4 cup sauce into the filling. Place generous 1/3 cup filling in the center of one tortilla; roll up to enclose the filling. Place enchilada in the baking dish. Repeat with remaining tortillas and filling.
5. Cover enchiladas with the remaining sauce, then sprinkle with the remaining 1/2 cup corn and 1/2 cup cheese.
6. Bake vegetable enchiladas until heated through, about 45 minutes. Sprinkle with 1/4 cup cilantro and serve.
